name: Pineapple Tart with Pina Colada Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 1 1/2 to 2 quart pan, mix 1/3 cup sugar, egg yolks, and 3/4 cup canned coconut milk. Stir over medium-low heat until the mixture thickly coats a metal spoon, about 10 minutes; do not boil. Stir in rum if using. Let cool completely. If made ahead, chill tightly covered for up to 1 day. Bring sauce to room temperature before serving.
2. On a lightly floured board, roll each pastry shell into an 8-inch round and trim the edges to neaten. Set rounds slightly apart on two baking sheets measuring 12x15 inches. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F until golden and crisp, about 10-15 minutes (switch pans halfway through baking). Cool pastries on racks.
3. Halve pineapples lengthwise; remove the core. Slice crosswise into 1/4-inch thick pieces. Place half of the pineapple slices on each baking sheet. Position one pan approximately 3 inches below the broiler and cook until lightly browned, about 10 minutes, then turn the pineapple over and sprinkle with 1 tablespoon sugar. Continue broiling until browned, about 6 minutes. Repeat with remaining fruit.
4. To serve, place half or all of a pastry on each dinner plate. Arrange pineapple slices evenly on the pastries. Pour coconut sauce around the desserts. Spoon dots of strawberry sauce onto the coconut sauce and garnish with mint sprigs if desired.
